title: Comforting Meatloaf and Creamy Mashed Potatoes for Dinner
promptIMG: Food photography: meatloaf and mashed potatoes, appetizing presentation, natural lighting, kitchen setting, documentary style, realistic colors --ar 4:3 --quality 2
description: This classic meatloaf paired with creamy mashed potatoes is pure comfort food. It's hearty, satisfying, and perfect for family dinners or cozy nights in!
instructions: In a large bowl, mix together the ground beef, breadcrumbs, onion, garlic, egg, milk, Worcestershire sauce, salt, and pepper until well combined.
Transfer the meat mixture into a loaf pan, shaping it into a loaf. Spread ketchup over the top for a nice glaze.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 50-60 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 160°F (70°C).
While the meatloaf is baking, boil the diced potatoes in salted water until tender, about 15-20 minutes.
Drain the potatoes and return them to the pot. Add butter, heavy cream, and season with salt and pepper. Mash until creamy.
Once the meatloaf is done, let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing. Serve with a generous scoop of mashed potatoes.
tips: For a flavor boost, consider adding some chopped fresh herbs like parsley or thyme to the meat mixture. A common mistake is overmixing the meatloaf, which can make it tough—mix just until combined! Leftover meatloaf makes for fantastic sandwiches the next day, so don't be afraid to make extra.

structured_content.content: Honestly, There's something so comforting about a classic meatloaf paired with creamy mashed potatoes. I remember my mom making this dish on chilly evenings, filling the house with the warm aroma of savory goodness. Meatloaf is one of those meals that brings everyone together around the dinner table, and it's easy to see why!

This meatloaf recipe is built on simple, hearty ingredients. Ground beef is the star here, but you could easily swap in ground turkey or chicken for a lighter option. The breadcrumbs help bind everything together, while the Worcestershire sauce adds a depth of flavor that makes each bite irresistible.

Making this meatloaf is straightforward and satisfying. You'll start by mixing the ingredients in a bowl—just be careful not to overwork the meat! While the meatloaf bakes, you can whip up the mashed potatoes, which should be creamy and buttery. The timing works out perfectly, so everything is hot and ready to serve together.

upload.context: Delicious meatloaf slice with creamy mashed potatoes on a plate

structured_content.content: This dish is perfect for family dinners, but it also works great for meal prep. You can make it ahead of time and reheat it for a quick weeknight meal. Plus, if you have any leftovers, they make for an amazing sandwich the next day!
